3. Highest salary and performance bonus in automotive sector.
4. Good networking within various vertical and supplier companies.
5. Overseas work experience opportunities at Japan.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Official working hours: 9:45 hrs, in actual, you work for ~10 to 11 hours per day excluding travel time. So, do not expect a good work life balance. You will have no time for any extra-curricular or your family except on Sat and Sun.
2. Bond period is very high (upto 4-6 yrs) if you choose for overseas experience at Japan.
3. Promotions are slow and almost at fixed rate. Min 3 yrs, on an average 4 yrs. However, now there are many designations to reach to a department head level.
4. Too much work pressure almost all the time.
5. Appraisal system is bad. Follows obsolete bell-curve system in which maximum (~85%) are appraised at middle grade. So, growth of person working hard and casual is almost same.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: As an MBA Graduate please refrain from applying to MSIL
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. Responsibility is good- Any MSIL outlet does good numbers so you will driving good volumes and revenues which you can shown on Resume.
2. Job Stability- The company has no fire policy, Salary will come on time, whether you work or don't work.
3. Extensive travelling- Outlets may not be located in same city, so upcountry travel is present with good allowances. After Covid this has come to a halt.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Legacy Company- Systems and processes are tedious. All decisions are rampant in bureaucracy and Red tapism. 
2. HR is absolutely weak- There is no Zonal or Regional HR team. There is a central HR Team sitting in Delhi HO. They don't know what happens in the region assigned. They conduct programs which doesn't add any value to the employee needs.
3. You will be transferred every 4 Years Minimum and you can apply for a role in different division after completing 3 Years in current role. Both the polices are subject to market conditions, your boss will not allow you to apply after 3 years in case you want to leave. They only care about their goals; you are a resource who they use as a stepping stone to do well in their career. 
4. No emphasis on Skills and Training- The company doesn't have a serious focus to skill their employees with new tools, technologies. There are knowledge sessions conducted by HR Team, these sessions are to please the top management in securing their KRA, the are portals such as E-gurukul and I-learn which gives basic knowledge but nothing else.
5. The work hours is 9 AM to 6:45 PM, there is a punching system even for the Sales force. People having completed the work before 6:45 PM are waiting to punch out from office. Their is an attendance report which is given to your manager mentioning at what time you have punched from where. If Sales are down don't be surprised if the report is sent to the team giving warning to punch from office. There have been instances where the boss has refused to give attendance or keeps you hanging to approve attendance. This attitude is dangerous and you might wonder forget about being a  manager, is he a human worth respecting.
6. Feedback is Zero- The performance appraisal is a scam. It happens twice a year, because of Covid there was no KRA set forget about any Mid term appraisal. You will be given a grade which depends on how much to say yes to your manager. There is absolutely no feedback provided throughout the year whether to improve you personally or professionally.
7. Decision Making is Archaic- Use of data to make decisions is missing. Appreciation for making decisions based on data is more so missing. Decisions are made to please the higher management. The only thing people say here is a "Yes Sir". "No" doesn't exist in Maruti Jargon. 
8. Specific to MBA grads, as knowledge workers the entire role will revolve around Sales operations which doesn't require an MBA degree. Switching to other industries is extremely difficult because Automotive Sales is not recognized as sales in the market. Within the same Industry MSIL pays the highest so other companies become out of bounds since you will have to take a pay cut to join most companies.
9. Missing Trust Factor- Your bosses will take calls for themselves and further their career at the expense of sidelining you. There is dual decision makers in the top with the Japanese and Indians, this to not to improve decision making but only gives more oversight and slows action.
10. Middle Management is empty- The senior Team is experienced at MSIL. The middle Management doesn't have any foresight, they are poor managers, there job revolves around pleasing the management.
11. In MSIL there is no people Management involved, people are Territory Sales Managers for 9-10 years without any experience of managing MSIL employees. As MBA's many industries provide People management skills and this is a key skill we want to add. If you come to MSIL this will be a huge detriment. 

Overall I might not be able to recall more cons, since there are ample. Please go through the pro's and con's carefully before making a call. This is specific to MBA' s however you can extrapolate this to other field roles such as service too where they get Graduate Engineer Trainees (GET). For the service team it is worse, so be aware.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Sarkaari Babu culture. Seniors are sometimes addressed as "Sahib" (Imagine that happening in 21st century). If “Sahib” makes a joke, everyone has to laugh. If “Sahib” said something, it is definitely a valid point - no questions asked.

2. Too much documentation and too many processes for little things. Example: You have to reject one part - get approval of 3 persons from 3 different departments along with their signatures! Just to reject one part?!

3. "Boss is Always Right" culture: If you are from the younger generation and have an open-minded attitude, then you're in for a shock. The boss culture is so prominent here.

4. Fresher exploitation at its peak

5. Passing the buck is very common.

6. Lack of professionalism in production/quality department.

7. Proper training not provided.

8. Egoistic management.

9. Department/work not allocated according to one's preference.

10. Bootlicking is expected and normal. Anyone who doesn't do that is humiliated and made to bootlick.

11. GETs are sometimes treated as slaves. Example: If there is suspicion of oil leakage is 10000 vehicles, GETs are told to go to the stockyard (in 50 degrees scorching heat!) and crawl under the vehicles and check the oil leakage in each and every one of the cars! This is what you studies engineering for!!!

12. Politics at its peak.

13. Top management ignores the concerns of new employees.

14. When you hand in your resignation, they pretend to be very sweet and caring to stop you (and probably harass you more later).

Don’t believe all of this? Keep in mind that we’re talking about a company where mismanagement is common to such an extent that a General Manager was burned alive on the company premises in 2012 (read about it through Google).
